The Princess of Wales is visiting children learning in the great Italian outdoors today on the second leg of her tour. Catherine has charmed Italians with her easy style and speaking their language learnt from her gap year days as a young woman. Today she is continuing her visit to Reggio Emilia, a northern city famed for its pioneering approach to early years education. She is seeing for herself how youngsters use nature-based learning at the Salvador Allende Scuola dell'infanzia school for under sixes. The school places particular emphasis on outdoor learning and environmental education.

Snooker star's mother and sister will be reported for £200,000 benefit fraud that emerged as they battled in court over £500,000 family home

NEW A professional snooker player's mother and sister are set to be reported after a family court feud ended with a judge finding they carried out a 19-year-long £200,000 benefits fraud. World No. 37 Matthew Selt and his sisters, Claire Noble and Charlotte Hamblin, were sued by their mother, Susan Hickenbotham, over claims they ganged up to 'coerce' her into signing away ownership of the £500,000 house in Essex, which had been her home since 1998. While the home was originally owned by the sportsman's father, Michael Selt, Ms Hickenbotham said she secured the property after their split in 2000 with a £7,500 down-payment, with her own father later clearing the remaining mortgage. But Central London County Court heard conflicting accounts of the home's legal standing as Ms Hickenbotham maintains that while her daughter, Ms Noble, holds the legal title, she remains the true beneficiary.
  • comments
  • share Snooker star's family reported for £200,000 benefits fraud

An 'absolutely stunning' four-bedroom home has hit the market for less than the price of a London flat - after it was converted from a subterranean Victorian reservoir. The unique property in Barnstaple, Devon is being auctioned with a guide price of between £400k and £450k. It boasts large arched brick ceilings over a reception hall as well as a kitchen, a sitting room, a utility room and three bathrooms. There is also ample outdoor space, with over half an acre of countryside, two garages and a Mediterranean-style brick-floored courtyard. Striking images show the traditional red-brick Victorian aesthetic complementing a modern interior.
  • 111 comments
  • share Underground water reservoir converted into unique home goes on market

With his dark hair, chiselled features and a toothy grin, Richard Baker thought he could cast a spell on any woman he crossed paths with in nightclubs in English seaside towns and holiday hotspots along the Costa del Sol. But in reality, there was nothing remotely charming about Baker, in fact hiding behind the suntan and flirtatious one-liners was one of the most dangerous sexual predators in British history. He committed a string of 'wicked and depraved' rapes while working as a part-time DJ, moving from resort-to-resort in London, Essex, Sussex and Spain, hunting new victims as he played pop music in sweaty bars and clubs and spiking their drinks with Rohypnol.

No it hasn't, it's just another manipulation exercise. You can't get on the waiting list for treatment because first you have to get an appointment with a GP, which you can't get or get fobbed off with a nurse practitioner. Then you have to see the physio, then you have to see the skeleton practitioner neither of which are available either. Then you may get a referral to a consultant. Add on another 6 months before you chase them and your 2 years down the line already before you even get to see a specialist and they have a lovely name for it...pathways. I really despise the NHS.

This is the dramatic moment an Indian man was catapulted 40ft into the air after the roof he was clinging to during a ferocious storm was torn off by gale force winds.  The man, named locally as Nanhe Miya, from Bareilly, in the state of Uttar Pradesh, miraculously survived the terrifying ordeal on Wednesday but sustained injuries after falling to the ground, according to Indian broadcaster NDTV.  Footage shared on social media purportedly shows Miya holding onto a tin roof when a powerful gust of wind suddenly blows over the structure, sending it flying.

Masked thugs wielding bats were forced to leave more than £100,000 in gold and silver behind during a botched jewellery shop heist after the quick-thinking owner set off a smoke bomb. A couple who turned a bunker into a bizarre second home have lost a planning battle after an inspector ruled it was missing one rather obvious feature - windows.  Nigel Barker and his wife Louise turned a former chemical treatment plant in picturesque Dartmoor into a 'peaceful' bolthole 200 miles away from their home in Crawley, West Sussex.  They purchased the Soda Ash building from South West Water in 2015 and had used the stark 1970s block as a private residence for eight years - often staying for weeks at a time.
